Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Milestone 4 — Firebase auth and cloud save (#11)

* feat: Milestone 4 — Firebase auth and cloud save

Adds anonymous sign-in, email/password account creation, and Firestore
cloud save so players can back up progress and restore it on a new device.

Changes:
- FirebaseAuth autoload: anonymous sign-in, token refresh, email/password
  sign-in and sign-up, account linking (anonymous → email), social login
  stubs (Apple/Google/Facebook show "Coming soon")
- CloudSave autoload: upload/download save document to Firestore REST API;
  stores entire save as a JSON string in a single Firestore field to avoid
  deeply nested type-map complexity
- SaveSystem: adds saved_at Unix timestamp to every save, emits
  local_save_completed signal, adds apply_cloud_data() for conflict resolution
- Main: connects auth_state_changed → CloudSave.download() on startup;
  on cloud download, picks newer save by saved_at; fires CloudSave.upload()
  after every local save (fire-and-forget)
- AuthScreen scene + script: email/password form, social login stubs,
  opened via "Account" button in UI
- game/data/firebase_config.json: placeholder config (user must fill in
  api_key and project_id from Firebase Console)
- backend/firestore.rules: security rules — users can only access their own
  document at users/{uid}/save/game
- project.godot: registers FirebaseAuth and CloudSave as autoloads

Social auth via native SDK is deferred to Milestone 4b.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: auth screen alignment, startup gate, and Play as Guest

Three bugs fixed:

1. AuthScreen was added to Main (Node2D) instead of \$UI (CanvasLayer),
   causing Controls to render in world space instead of screen space.
   Now added to the CanvasLayer so it centers correctly on screen.

2. FirebaseAuth auto-signed in anonymously on startup with no user prompt.
   Now emits needs_auth signal when no stored token exists, and Main wires
   that to show AuthScreen before the player can proceed.

3. No "Play as Guest" option existed. Added GuestButton that calls
   FirebaseAuth.sign_in_as_guest() (anonymous sign-in). AuthScreen
   closes automatically when auth_state_changed fires.

AuthScreen also gains a semi-transparent overlay blocker, a Close button
(only visible when opened from the Account button, not on first launch),
and proper form enable/disable during async auth calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Terraform for Firebase infrastructure provisioning

Adds backend/terraform/ with HCL configs that provision the entire Firebase
backend from a single `terraform apply`:
- GCP project + 6 required APIs
- Firebase project + web app registration
- Firestore database (native mode, us-central)
- Identity Platform: anonymous + email/password auth
- Firestore security rules deployment
- Writes game/data/firebase_config.json with real api_key/project_id

Replaces the previous manual Firebase Console + CLI setup flow.
Updates backend/README.md with Terraform usage instructions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add bootstrap script for Service Usage API chicken-and-egg

On a brand-new GCP project, Terraform cannot enable APIs because the
Service Usage API itself isn't enabled yet. bootstrap.sh breaks the
cycle with a one-time gcloud call before the first terraform apply.

Also documents the prerequisite step in backend/README.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct Firestore region from us-central to us-central1

us-central is not a valid Firestore location ID. Valid single-region
values include us-central1, us-east1, etc. Multi-region: nam5 (US),
eur3 (EU).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: skip anonymous linking on Create Account — use signUp directly

Identity Platform blocks accounts:update (anonymous→email link) with
OPERATION_NOT_ALLOWED when email verification is required. Simplify by
always calling accounts:signUp for new email accounts. Local save data
persists on device and uploads to the new account's cloud doc after
sign-up, so no progress is lost.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address critical and high security findings from audit

Critical:
- Commit root .gitignore with game/data/firebase_config.json excluded —
  prevents the real Firebase API key (written by Terraform) from ever
  entering git history
- Remove game/data/firebase_config.json from git tracking (git rm --cached);
  file remains on disk, gitignore prevents re-addition

High:
- Add GCS remote backend config to main.tf so terraform.tfstate (which
  contains the SCRYPT password signer key) is stored in GCS instead of
  local disk; adds google_storage_bucket.tf_state resource
- Update bootstrap.sh with guided 3-step state migration flow
- Fix backend/terraform/.gitignore: clarify .terraform.lock.hcl status,
  keep *.tfstate excluded

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use project_id prefix for GCS state bucket; update system prompt

- GCS bucket names are globally unique; fixed name caused 409 conflict.
  Switching to ${var.project_id}-tf-state ensures uniqueness per project.
- Update CLAUDE_SYSTEM_PROMPT.md with simplified repo structure section.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Lift shared relationship logic from UserHandler/RoleHandler into AbstractHandler

Both handlers contained identical copies of 7 relationship methods plus a
RelationshipEntry inner class and two fields (relationshipCollections,
readOnlyRelationships). This duplication caused them to drift — the recent
enrichWithRelationships bugfix in RoleHandler is a direct consequence.

Moved to AbstractHandler (verbatim from UserHandler, the reference impl):
- enrichWithRelationships (protected)
- syncRelationship (protected)
- fetchRelationshipIds, fetchCurrentRelationships (private)
- applyRelationshipAdd, applyRelationshipRemove (private)
- RelationshipEntry inner class (private static)
- relationshipCollections and readOnlyRelationships fields (protected final)

Both handler constructors now forward these fields to super(). All LOG.debug
calls from UserHandler are preserved. No logic changes beyond access modifier
widening (private → protected) for the two methods called from subclasses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: cross-repo setup guide + peer machine setup complete

Reproducible 5-step setup guide using placeholder variables (no
machine-specific details). Peer machine (psq-agent) verified:
identity, remote, state.db, hooks, cron all operational.
Bidirectional transport confirmed.

commit.message:
Release v0.3.0: Phase 2 Complete - Vision Models, Function Calling, and Test Coverage

## Major Features
- Vision Models Support: Image analysis across Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google providers
- Function Calling / Tool Use: Tool definitions and execution for all major providers
- Test Coverage: Increased from 35% to 76% coverage with 381 new tests

## Test Coverage Achievements
- 381 new tests across 5 provider test suites and 4 feature test modules
- Provider unit tests: BaseProvider, Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Ollama
- Feature tests: Streaming (46 tests), Client Integration (31), Edge Cases (37), Vision & Tools (28)
- All tests mock external dependencies - no API keys required

## CI/CD Improvements
- Smart test matrix: Fast PR testing, comprehensive main branch testing
- Quality workflows: Lint, type-check, security scanning with Bandit
- Coverage enforcement: 70%+ threshold in CI
- Dependabot integration for automated dependency updates

## Documentation Updates
- New docs/vision.md: Complete Vision Models Guide
- New docs/function-calling.md: Complete Function Calling Guide
- Updated README.md with Vision and Function Calling sections
- Created CHANGELOG.md with comprehensive release notes
- Updated pyproject.toml: v0.2.0 → v0.3.0
- Contact email: Hermes_creative@proton.me

## New Examples
- examples/12_vision_models.py: Vision models demonstration
- examples/13_function_calling.py: Function calling demonstration

## Backward Compatibility
- All Phase 1 functionality remains unchanged
- Existing code continues to work unchanged
- New features are opt-in additions

only v5.4.624 from @types/pdfjs-dist was present)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Restore discord_listener with full functionality + seed overnight gap

Archi's auto-generated rewrite of discord_listener.py lost all critical
functionality: intent classification (GAP/REQUEST/CONVERSATION/TRIGGER),
gap logging to operation_log, image handling, discord_notify for sending
responses, and the SYSTEM_PROMPT. If the daemon restarted with that
version, Archi would process messages but never reply.

Restored the definitive version with:
- Full intent classification and gap logging
- Image attachment handling via vision pipeline
- discord_notify for all response paths
- Conversational memory integration (store + context in prompts)
- Recall command support (!recall, !nearest, !messages)
- Graceful fallbacks for optional capabilities

Also seeded overnight_self_improvement gap so Archi can spend tonight
reflecting and building toward Jesse's six dimensions while he sleeps.

Also includes Archi's auto-generated capabilities from this session:
timestamped_chat_history_recall, gap_logging_sync, and their wiring.

117/117 tests passing.

commit.message:
fix(security): sanitize AGE HTTP 500 errors and validate graph_name (#75)

Two security issues in foundry-graph-age:

1. Information disclosure: all six HTTP 500 error paths returned raw
   DB/Cypher error strings (e.g. "db error", AGE internals, graph
   structure details). Fixed by logging to stderr and returning a
   generic "internal server error" to callers — same pattern as PR #73.

2. graph_name injection: Configure() accepted any string for graph_name,
   which is interpolated unescaped into every Cypher query template via
   fmt.Sprintf. A misconfigured or adversarially-supplied graph_name
   containing special characters (quotes, semicolons, spaces) could
   inject Cypher. Fixed by applying the existing reValidLabel regex
   (^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_]*$) to graph_name during Configure().

Test changes:
- Six existing DB-error tests updated to assert body contains
  "internal server error" and does NOT contain the raw error string
- TestConfigure_ValidGraphName: valid names accepted
- TestConfigure_InvalidGraphName_RejectsInjection: injection-style
  names rejected

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Duane Cutlip headshot (duane-cutlip.png)

author bylines.

n8n does not handle CORS preflight (OPTIONS) for PATCH/PUT/DELETE methods —
returns 500 with no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header.

the n8n webhook which now listens on POST /api/v2/vacancy/update.

and Number of Releases widgets from populating on the Sentry dashboard.

commit.message:
refactor: MCP-first state management, SQLite as single source of truth

Signals, context, and run metadata now flow exclusively through MCP
tools to SQLite. Removes filesystem dual-write (signals/, context.md,
run.json). MCP server gains get_context and get_run_info tools so
agents query orchestration state via MCP instead of reading files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix CORS default to explicit localhost origins; harden all five fixes

CORS (main gap remaining from prior session):
- settings.py: ALLOWED_ORIGINS now defaults to
  "http://localhost:3000,http://localhost:8000" instead of blank/wildcard.
  ALLOWED_ORIGINS is always a list of explicit origins, so
  allow_credentials=True is always valid — no wildcard is ever used.
- settings.py: removed derived CORS_ALLOW_CREDENTIALS flag (no longer needed).
- main.py: CORS middleware now uses allow_origins=ALLOWED_ORIGINS directly
  with allow_credentials=True unconditionally. No wildcard fallback.
- .env.example: ALLOWED_ORIGINS shown with the localhost default; production
  override instructions added.

Remaining fixes (verified correct, comments and docs clarified):
- routes.py: citations always built from retrieval results before generation;
  _has_inline_citations dependency fully removed; HTTPException(502) raised
  on retrieval and generation failure; SSE emits event:error on failure.
- chat.py: ENABLE_IN_MEMORY_HISTORY=false (default) makes _get_history return
  [] and _save_turn a no-op; every request is fully stateless by default.
- _check_gate: evaluates top chunk score (not average); MIN_RESULTS=1 so one
  strong result is sufficient.
- README.md: CORS section rewritten to match actual code behavior; all other
  sections updated to match corrected implementation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(soul): self-evolving shared soul with reflection, exploration, and tension resolution

The shared soul (app/SOUL.md) is no longer static — it grows through three
system-level scheduler jobs:

- soul-reflection (weekly): reads per-workspace SOUL.md diffs, extracts
  abstract patterns, identifies value tensions, may refine shared soul
- soul-exploration (twice/week): autonomous curiosity via WebSearch,
  forms own opinions in INTERESTS.md
- soul-tension-review (monthly): resolves accumulated tensions between
  per-SOUL evolution and shared soul values (Refine/Reaffirm/Defer)

Token optimization: pre-collects only changed per-SOULs via content hash
snapshots + line diff, so AI only sees new/modified content.

Privacy: reflection jobs never read user data (USER.md, MEMORY.md,
conversations). TENSIONS.md uses anonymous keys, no user identifiers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: inject sender entity_id into memory_store_fact via routing context [bu-e0g.4] (#529)

When route.execute processes a request with source_sender_entity_id in
the request_context, the entity_id is now injected into _routing_ctx_var
before spawner.trigger() runs. The spawner captures this into the
per-session _runtime_routing_context. memory_store_fact reads
source_entity_id from the runtime session routing context as the default
entity_id when the LLM does not supply one explicitly.

Explicit entity_id from the LLM always takes precedence. Works for both
owner and third-party sender messages.
